studies.officialTitle: STEP 1: Research Study Investigating How Well Semaglutide Works in People Suffering From Overweight or Obesity
studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with at least one weight-related comorbidity, without diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: Gastrointestinal disorders were the most frequent adverse events and were generally mild to moderate; nausea occurred in 44.2% with semaglutide and 17.4% with placebo, vomiting in 24.8% and 6.6%, and serious adverse events in 9.8% and 6.4%, respectively.

sources.title: Once-Weekly Semaglutide in Adults with Overweight or Obesity

studies.officialTitle: Research Study Investigating How Well Semaglutide Works in People With Type 2 Diabetes Suffering From Overweight or Obesity
studies.population: Adults with obesity or overweight and type 2 diabetes; weight management was an explicit objective.
studies.safetySummary: Gastrointestinal adverse events were most frequent; nausea occurred in 33.7% with semaglutide 2.4 mg, 32.1% with semaglutide 1.0 mg, and 9.2% with placebo, vomiting in 21.8%, 13.4%, and 2.7%, and serious adverse events in 9.9%, 7.7%, and 9.2%, respectively.

sources.title: Semaglutide 2.4 mg once a week in adults with overweight or obesity, and type 2 diabetes

studies.officialTitle: Two-year Research Study Investigating How Well Semaglutide Works in People Suffering From Overweight or Obesity

design.description: Two-year parallel-group trial with behavioral intervention.

studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with at least one weight-related comorbidity, without diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: Serious adverse events occurred in 7.9% and 11.8%, nausea in 53.3% and 21.7%, and vomiting in 30.3% and 4.6% with semaglutide and placebo, respectively.

sources.title: Two-year effects of semaglutide in adults with overweight or obesity

studies.officialTitle: A Research Study on How Well Semaglutide Works in Adolescents With Overweight or Obesity
studies.population: Adolescents aged 12 to under 18 years with obesity, or overweight plus a weight-related condition.
studies.safetySummary: Serious adverse events occurred in 11% and 9%, nausea in 42% and 18%, and vomiting in 36% and 10% with semaglutide and placebo, respectively.

sources.title: Once-Weekly Semaglutide in Adolescents with Obesity

studies.officialTitle: A Research Study to See How Well Semaglutide Helps People With Excess Body Weight Lose Weight
studies.population: Adults with obesity without diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: Serious adverse events occurred in 6.8%, 10.9%, and 5.5%, nausea in 43.7%, 38.3%, and 12.9%, and vomiting in 24.8%, 16.4%, and 7.0% with semaglutide 7.2 mg, semaglutide 2.4 mg, and placebo, respectively.

sources.title: Once-weekly semaglutide 7.2 mg in adults with obesity

studies.officialTitle: A Research Study to See How Well Semaglutide Helps People With Type 2 Diabetes and Excess Body Weight Lose Weight
studies.population: Adults with obesity and type 2 diabetes; weight management was an explicit objective.
studies.safetySummary: Serious adverse events occurred in 9.1%, 8.7%, and 8.8%, nausea in 29.0%, 28.2%, and 11.8%, and vomiting in 16.6%, 13.6%, and 2.9% with semaglutide 7.2 mg, semaglutide 2.4 mg, and placebo, respectively.

sources.title: Once-weekly semaglutide 7.2 mg in adults with obesity and type 2 diabetes

studies.officialTitle: A Research Study to See How Well Oral Semaglutide Helps People With Overweight or Obesity Lose Weight
studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with at least one weight-related comorbidity, without diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: Serious adverse events occurred in 3.9% and 8.8%, nausea in 46.6% and 18.6%, and vomiting in 30.9% and 5.9% with oral semaglutide 25 mg and placebo, respectively.

sources.title: Oral Semaglutide at a Dose of 25 mg in Adults with Overweight or Obesity

studies.officialTitle: Effect and Safety of Semaglutide 2.4 mg Once-weekly as Adjunct to Intensive Behavioural Therapy in Subjects With Overweight or Obesity

design.description: Parallel-group trial with intensive behavioural therapy and an initial low-calorie diet.

studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with at least one weight-related comorbidity, without diabetes.

sources.title: Effect of Subcutaneous Semaglutide vs Placebo as an Adjunct to Intensive Behavioral Therapy on Body Weight in Adults With Overweight or Obesity: The STEP 3 Randomized Clinical Trial

studies.officialTitle: Effect and Safety of Semaglutide 2.4 mg Once-weekly in Subjects With Overweight or Obesity Who Have Reached Target Dose During run-in Period

design.description: After a 20-week semaglutide run-in, participants continued semaglutide 2.4 mg or switched to placebo for 48 weeks.

studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with at least one weight-related comorbidity, without diabetes, who reached semaglutide 2.4 mg during run-in.

sources.title: Effect of Continued Weekly Subcutaneous Semaglutide vs Placebo on Weight Loss Maintenance in Adults With Overweight or Obesity: The STEP 4 Randomized Clinical Trial

studies.officialTitle: Effect and Safety of Semaglutide Once-weekly in East Asian Subjects With Overweight or Obesity

design.description: Four-arm parallel-group trial of semaglutide 2.4 mg, semaglutide 1.7 mg, and their dose-matched placebos.

studies.population: East Asian adults with obesity or overweight.

sources.title: Semaglutide once a week in adults with overweight or obesity, with or without type 2 diabetes in an east Asian population (STEP 6): a randomised, double-blind, double-dummy, placebo-controlled, phase 3a trial

studies.officialTitle: Effect and Safety of Semaglutide 2.4 mg Once-weekly on Weight Management in Subjects With Overweight or Obesity.

design.description: Parallel-group trial with diet and physical-activity counselling.

studies.population: Adults with obesity or overweight, with or without type 2 diabetes.

sources.title: Efficacy and safety of once weekly semaglutide 2.4 mg for weight management in a predominantly east Asian population with overweight or obesity (STEP 7): a double-blind, multicentre, randomised controlled trial

studies.officialTitle: Effect and Safety of Subcutaneous Semaglutide 2.4 mg Once Weekly Compared to Liraglutide 3.0 mg Once Daily on Weight Management in Subjects With Overweight or Obesity

design.description: Four-arm parallel-group trial; semaglutide-versus-liraglutide assignment was open-label, with each active treatment double-blinded against its dosing-frequency-matched placebo.

studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with at least one weight-related comorbidity, without diabetes.

sources.title: Effect of Weekly Subcutaneous Semaglutide vs Daily Liraglutide on Body Weight in Adults With Overweight or Obesity Without Diabetes: The STEP 8 Randomized Clinical Trial

studies.officialTitle: Effect of Subcutaneous Semaglutide 2.4 mg Once-weekly Compared to Placebo in Subjects With Obesity and Knee Osteoarthritis

design.description: Parallel-group trial with reduced-calorie diet and increased physical activity.

studies.population: Adults with obesity and knee osteoarthritis.

sources.title: Once-Weekly Semaglutide in Persons with Obesity and Knee Osteoarthritis

studies.officialTitle: Efficacy and Safety of Subcutaneous Semaglutide 2.4 mg Once-weekly in Subjects With Obesity and Prediabetes

design.description: Parallel-group trial with reduced-calorie diet and increased physical activity.

studies.population: Adults with obesity and prediabetes.

sources.title: Efficacy and safety of once-weekly semaglutide 2.4 mg versus placebo in people with obesity and prediabetes (STEP 10): a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled, multicentre phase 3 trial

studies.officialTitle: Efficacy and Safety of Semaglutide 2.4 mg Once-weekly in Adults With Overweight and Obesity
studies.population: Adults with obesity or overweight.

studies.officialTitle: Effect of Semaglutide 2.4 mg Once Weekly on Function and Symptoms in Subjects With Obesity-related Heart Failure With Preserved Ejection Fraction

design.description: Parallel-group trial with study treatment added to standard care.

studies.population: Adults with obesity-related heart failure with preserved ejection fraction, without diabetes, receiving standard care.

sources.title: Semaglutide in Patients with Heart Failure with Preserved Ejection Fraction and Obesity

studies.officialTitle: Effect of Semaglutide 2.4 mg Once-weekly on Function and Symptoms in Subjects With Obesity-related Heart Failure With Preserved Ejection Fraction, and Type 2 Diabetes
studies.population: Adults with obesity-related heart failure with preserved ejection fraction and type 2 diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: During the treatment period, serious adverse events occurred in 17.7% of participants receiving semaglutide and 28.8% receiving placebo.

sources.title: Semaglutide in Patients with Heart Failure with Preserved Ejection Fraction and Obesity with Type 2 Diabetes

studies.officialTitle: Weight Maintenance in Adolescents With Obesity; Long-Term Treatment With Semaglutide s.c.2.4 mg Once-weekly
studies.population: Adolescents aged 12 to 15 years with obesity.

sources.title: STEP TEENS Weight Maintenance study record

studies.officialTitle: Efficacy and Safety of Oral Semaglutide 50 mg Once Daily in Subjects With Overweight or Obesity (OASIS 1)
studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with at least one weight-related comorbidity, without diabetes.

sources.title: Oral semaglutide 50 mg taken once per day in adults with overweight or obesity (OASIS 1): a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled, phase 3 trial

studies.officialTitle: Efficacy and Safety of Oral Semaglutide 50 mg Once Daily in East Asian Participants With Overweight or Obesity
studies.population: East Asian adults with obesity or overweight.
studies.safetySummary: During the on-treatment period, serious adverse events occurred in 6.0% and 9.1%, nausea in 25.4% and 7.6%, and vomiting in 14.9% and 3.0% with oral semaglutide 50 mg and placebo, respectively.

sources.title: Oral Semaglutide in an East Asian Population With Overweight or Obesity, With or Without Type 2 Diabetes

studies.officialTitle: Efficacy and Safety of Oral Semaglutide 50 mg Once Daily in Chinese Adults With Overweight or Obesity (OASIS 3)
studies.population: Chinese adults with obesity or overweight, with or without type 2 diabetes.

studies.officialTitle: SELECT - Semaglutide Effects on Cardiovascular Outcomes in People With Overweight or Obesity

design.description: Parallel-group cardiovascular outcomes trial with treatment added to standard care.

studies.population: Adults aged 45 years or older with overweight or obesity and established cardiovascular disease, without diabetes.

sources.title: Semaglutide and Cardiovascular Outcomes in Obesity without Diabetes
